codeReview.summary: The rerun does resolve the prior semantic blocker: `prepare_input_with_session` now seeds internal-only empty-object identity before callback matching (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:41-42`), `json_value_identity` uses that identity for `{}` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:223-226`), and the new regression proves a callback-returned history-side `{}` is no longer persisted as fresh input (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:395-417`) while the prior new-turn regression still exists (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:440-442`). The public `InputItem` shape also remains unchanged (`crates/agents-core/src/items.rs:10-12`) and the facade regression still exercises downstream construction/pattern matching without provenance fields (`crates/openai-agents/tests/openai_session_semantics.rs:115-179`). However, the fix derives empty-object identity by hashing raw bytes from `serde_json::Map` through `unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(...) }` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:260-271`). That relies on private in-memory layout bytes being a valid, stable identity source, which is not sound Rust and makes the shipped fix depend on undefined-behavior-prone implementation details.

issues.description: `empty_json_object_identity` hashes the raw bytes of `serde_json::Map` via `unsafe { std::slice::from_raw_parts(...) }` to distinguish duplicate empty objects. Reading an arbitrary struct as initialized bytes is not a safe or stable identity source: the type's layout is private, may contain padding/uninitialized bytes, and can change across compiler or `serde_json` versions. Because `json_value_identity` now routes every callback-owned empty object through this helper (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:223-226`), the fix introduces a correctness/safety bug even though the new history-side regression passes. This should use a safe internal sidecar identity instead of hashing struct bytes.

summary: Reviewed the fix feature transcript skeleton, the original failed diff, the follow-up diff, the prior review, and the relevant shared-state docs. The rerun fixes the original history-side `{}` misattribution without changing the public `InputItem` API, but it still fails scrutiny because the new internal identity mechanism depends on unsafe hashing of `serde_json::Map`'s raw memory representation.
